name: Flank Steak with Garlic Wine Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle flank steak with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
2. Heat a wide heavy skillet without adding any fat.
3. When hot, cook the seasoned steak until it is seared and well browned on each side (about 1 minute per side).
4. Reduce heat and add 2 tablespoons butter. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes on each side.
5. For best results, the meat should be quite rare. Remove the meat from the pan and keep warm.
6. Pour off the fat in the skillet and add the scallions and red wine. Bring to a boil and whisk in the garlic puree.
7. Boil until the wine is reduced by half, thickened, and syrupy.
8. As it boils, scrape up the browned bits in the skillet with a wooden spoon.
9. Stir in the meat juices that have accumulated under the flank steak.
10. Boil for 1 or 2 seconds more.
11. Remove from the heat. Gently swirl in the softened butter so that it incorporates into the wine.
12. Quickly slice the meat, against the grain, into thin strips.
13. Arrange the slices on a hot platter, pour sauce down the center of them and serve at once.
